messages.content:
Rewrite these sentences using:.........., after which/ ............, with.......
 
"The percentage of positions in parliament held by women in this country was about 34%, followed by a gradual rise to 36% in 2012."
The percentage of positions in parliament held by women in this country was about 34%, after which there was a gradual rise to 36% in 2012.

Rewrite these sentences using: followed by/ with
"The proportion of females elected for parliament in France started at 25%, after which it had reached a peak at around 37% by 2004, before ending at 32%."
The proportion of females elected for parliament in France started at 25%, followed by a peak of around 37% by 2004, before ending with 32%.

Rewrite these sentences using: similarly "The percentage of positions in parliament held by women in Germany was about 34%, followed by a gradual rise to 36% in 2012. Similar changes, but to greater extents, can be seen when we look at the figures for Italy and the UK, which grew from 26% to almost 40% and from 4% to around 27% respectively."

The percentage of positions in parliament held by women in Germany was about 34%, followed by a gradual rise to 36% in 2012. Similarly, when we look at the figures for Italy and the UK, we can see similar changes but to greater extents. In Italy, the percentage grew from 26% to almost 40%, while in the UK it grew from 4% to around 27%.

Write me a band 9 IELTS Writing task 2 for this question : Writing, reading and maths are the three recognized traditional subjects. Computer skills should also be the fourth largest branch. Do you agree or disagree?

In today's technologically advanced world, there is an ongoing debate regarding the inclusion of computer skills as the fourth major subject alongside the traditional subjects of writing, reading, and maths. While some argue that computer skills are essential in preparing students for the future, others believe that the current curriculum suffices. In my opinion, I strongly agree that computer skills should be recognized as the fourth largest branch.

First and foremost, we live in an era where computers play an integral role in our everyday lives. From communication to information retrieval, the digital age demands individuals who possess adequate computer literacy. By incorporating computer skills into the academic curriculum, students will be equipped with the vital knowledge and abilities necessary for success in both their personal and professional lives. Consequently, this integration will not only bridge the gap between education and real-life application but also prepare students to navigate the increasingly technology-driven world.

Furthermore, the inclusion of computer skills as a major subject would foster innovation and creativity among students. Computers provide a platform for endless possibilities, stimulating critical thinking, problem-solving, and analytical skills. By teaching students the fundamentals of coding, programming, and explore various software applications, they will develop an innovative mindset, expanding their horizons beyond traditional subjects. This shift will enable students to adapt to the rapidly evolving job market, where technology is a driving force in all industries.

On the other hand, it can be argued that the current curriculum adequately covers computer skills within existing subjects or as elective courses. However, the occasional exposure to computers does not equip students with comprehensive knowledge in this field. With an independent branch dedicated to computer skills, students would receive the necessary expertise and in-depth understanding to navigate the complexities of today's digital world.

In conclusion, I firmly believe that computer skills should be recognized as the fourth largest branch alongside the traditional subjects of writing, reading, and maths. By incorporating computer literacy into the curriculum, students will be better prepared for the demands of the modern era, equipped with the essential skills and knowledge required for success. It is imperative to adapt the education system to reflect the evolving technological landscape, ensuring that future generations thrive in the digital age.
Write me a band 9 IELTS Writing task 2 for this question : Everyone should stay at school until 18. To what extent do you agree or disagree?

The question of whether everyone should stay at school until the age of 18 is a topic that has sparked considerable debate. While some argue that it is necessary for every individual to complete their education until this age, others voice concerns regarding personal choice and individual circumstances. In my view, I firmly agree that everyone should be mandated to stay in school until the age of 18 for several compelling reasons.

Firstly, education is the key to unlocking opportunities and creating a successful future. By extending the compulsory schooling age, individuals are provided with a solid foundation of knowledge and skills that can significantly enhance their employability prospects. In today's competitive job market, a high school diploma is often the minimum requirement for many entry-level positions. Therefore, by promoting a longer educational experience, individuals are better equipped to meet the demands of the workforce and secure stable employment.

Furthermore, staying in school until 18 ensures that individuals are exposed to a well-rounded education. School is not only a place for acquiring academic knowledge but also serves as a platform for personal development and growth. Through engagement in extracurricular activities, interaction with peers, and exposure to diverse subjects, students have the opportunity to discover their passions and talents. By encouraging students to stay in school until 18, we allow them sufficient time and resources to explore various subjects, develop their skills, and make more informed decisions about their future academic and career paths.

Moreover, extended schooling instills critical life skills that are essential for adulthood. By remaining in an educational environment until the age of 18, students cultivate important skills such as time management, teamwork, and effective communication. These skills are not only valuable in academic settings but also transcend into various aspects of life, including relationships, professional settings, and personal growth. Therefore, the longer individuals stay in school, the greater their exposure to these vital life skills, ensuring their holistic development.

Admittedly, there are individuals who may argue against extending compulsory education until the age of 18, emphasizing the importance of personal choice and readiness for the workforce. While these concerns hold some merit, it is crucial to recognize that education is a fundamental right that should be provided to everyone. By imposing this requirement, we ensure that all individuals have equal access to education, regardless of their socio-economic background or personal circumstances. Moreover, by fostering a culture where education is prioritized until the age of 18, we can combat the issue of early school leaving, which is often associated with negative societal consequences such as increased unemployment rates and reduced opportunities for personal growth.

In conclusion, I firmly believe that it is crucial for everyone to stay at school until the age of 18. By doing so, individuals are provided with ample opportunity to acquire essential knowledge, develop crucial life skills, and explore their interests. Education is the cornerstone of personal and societal growth, and by mandating extended schooling, we ensure that individuals are well-prepared to navigate the challenges and opportunities that lie ahead.

Write me a band 9 IELTS Writing task 2 for this question: Do you agree or disagree with the statement that the government should invest more money in science education rather than other subjects to develop the country?

The allocation of government funds towards different subjects in education is a matter of considerable deliberation. While some argue that the government should prioritize investment in science education to foster national development, others advocate for a more balanced approach, highlighting the importance of other subjects. In my opinion, while science education is undeniably crucial, equal emphasis should be placed on all subjects to ensure comprehensive development and progress within the country.

On one hand, advocates for increased investment in science education argue that it is the driving force behind innovation and technological advancements. In an increasingly competitive global landscape, advancements in science and technology play a pivotal role in economic growth, job creation, and societal development. By investing more money in science education, the government can cultivate a skilled workforce that is capable of tackling complex scientific challenges, contributing to groundbreaking research, and developing innovative solutions to address various issues faced by the country.

Moreover, a strong foundation in science education equips individuals with critical thinking, problem-solving, and analytical skills — attributes that are highly valued in a rapidly evolving world. Exposure to scientific principles and concepts at an early stage promotes curiosity, fosters a spirit of inquiry, and nurtures a scientific mindset. These attributes not only enable individuals to understand and appreciate the world around them but also spark creativity and innovation, driving progress and growth in diverse sectors.

However, it is essential to acknowledge that a well-rounded education encompasses more than just science. Allocating sufficient resources to other subjects such as humanities, literature, arts, and social sciences is equally vital in nurturing a society that is well-informed, culturally enriched, and socially aware. These subjects cultivate empathy, critical thinking, and societal understanding, fostering a harmonious and inclusive society. Moreover, neglecting the humanities in favor of science education may inadvertently lead to an imbalance in society, as it fails to recognize the value of diverse perspectives, creative expression, and humanistic contributions to the progress of the country.

Furthermore, a comprehensive education system should cater to the diverse needs and interests of students. Not all individuals possess a natural affinity or inclination towards science. By offering a wide range of subjects, students can explore their true passions, discover their unique talents, and develop their respective areas of expertise. This inclusivity ensures that the country benefits from a diverse pool of talent, encompassing not only scientists and engineers but also artists, writers, social scientists, and individuals with varied skill sets.

In conclusion, although investments in science education are crucial for national progress, a balanced approach that encompasses all subjects is imperative. The government should recognize the inherent value and contributions of each subject area. By providing equal resources and opportunities to science as well as other subjects, the country can foster a well-rounded, skilled, and innovative population that fuels progress and development across various sectors. An education system that embraces the harmonious coexistence of science, humanities, and other subjects is fundamental in shaping a thriving and enlightened society.

Write me a band 9 IELTS Writing task 2 for this question: Do you agree or disagree with the statement that the government should invest more money in science education rather than other subjects to develop the country?

The question of allocating government funds towards various subjects in education to foster national development is a contentious issue. Some argue that the government should prioritize investing more money in science education, while others advocate for a more balanced approach, emphasizing the importance of other subjects. In my opinion, I believe that the government should indeed invest more money in science education compared to other subjects to promote the overall development and progress of the country.

Firstly, science education plays a crucial role in driving innovation, technological advancements, and economic growth. In today's rapidly evolving world, scientific knowledge and expertise are vital for a country to remain competitive on the global stage. By investing more money in science education, the government can foster the development of skilled scientists, engineers, and researchers who can contribute to groundbreaking discoveries and technological advancements. This, in turn, attracts foreign investments, promotes industrial growth, and creates employment opportunities, ultimately spurring the country's overall development.

Moreover, science education equips individuals with critical thinking, analytical skills, and problem-solving abilities. These skills are not only valuable within the scientific realm but are also highly transferable to other fields. Investing in science education ensures that individuals possess a strong foundation in scientific principles, allowing them to apply logical reasoning and evidence-based decision-making in various aspects of life. These attributes are essential in addressing complex societal challenges, making informed judgments, and contributing to overall societal progress.

Furthermore, focusing on science education aligns with the ever-increasing reliance on technology and scientific advancements in various sectors. Fields such as healthcare, environmental sustainability, energy, and transportation require individuals with a solid understanding of scientific concepts and methodologies. By prioritizing science education, the government ensures that the country possesses a well-equipped workforce capable of addressing pressing issues and providing innovative solutions in these crucial areas. This, in turn, leads to sustainable development, improved living standards, and a prosperous nation.

However, it is important to acknowledge the value of a well-rounded education that includes other subjects such as arts, humanities, and social sciences. These subjects are integral in fostering creativity, critical thinking, cultural awareness, and societal understanding. Neglecting these subjects entirely can hinder the development of a holistic society. Therefore, while it is crucial to invest more in science education, the government should also allocate reasonable funds to ensure the availability and quality of other subjects to provide students with a well-rounded education.

In conclusion, prioritizing investment in science education compared to other subjects is essential for the development and progress of the country. Science education drives innovation, economic growth, and technological advancements, while also equipping individuals with crucial skills applicable in various domains. However, a balanced approach must be maintained, ensuring that other subjects are not overlooked, as they contribute to the overall development of a well-rounded society. By investing in science education while also recognizing the value of other subjects, the government can pave the way for a prosperous and advanced nation.

Enhance my introduction:
Some people believe that that more money should be invested in science education rather than other subjects for the development of the country. Personally, I completely agree with this view due to following reasons.

Some people hold the belief that allocating a larger portion of financial resources towards science education, as opposed to other subjects, is crucial for the progress and development of a nation. From my perspective, I completely concur with this standpoint, as I believe that investing more funds in science education offers numerous advantages and opportunities that can propel the country towards a brighter future.

Write similar introductions for these questions: 
 It is better for college students to live in schools than live at home with their parents. Do you agree or disagree?
 
 

In schools and universities, girls tend to choose arts, while boys like science. What are the reasons for this trend and do you think this tendency should be changed?
 
 

It is neither possible nor useful for a country to provide university places for a high proportion of young people. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
 
 

Some universities offer online courses as an alternative to classes delivered on campus. Do you think this is a positive or negative development?

1. Personally, I am of the opinion that it is more advantageous for college students to reside within the school premises rather than live at home with their parents. This issue has sparked debates among individuals, and in my view, the advantages of living in schools outweigh the benefits of staying at home.

2. In educational institutions, a noticeable trend is observed where girls commonly opt for arts courses while boys gravitate towards science subjects. The underlying reasons for this inclination and whether this tendency should be altered are topics of significant discussion. From my perspective, exploring the factors driving this trend and evaluating the necessity for change is crucial in promoting gender equality and facilitating students' individual aspirations.

3. The prospect of a country accommodating a high proportion of young people in universities is a topic that evokes debate. While providing university places to a large number of young individuals may seem unattainable or impractical, the extent of agreement or disagreement with this notion varies among individuals. In my opinion, carefully evaluating the feasibility and benefits of such a provision is vital to ensure an optimal approach to higher education for the country's youth.

4. With the emergence of online learning platforms, universities are now offering virtual courses as an alternative to traditional classroom-based education. The impact of this development has sparked conversations surrounding its advantages and disadvantages. From my perspective, assessing the implications of online courses and determining whether this is a positive or negative trend is crucial in understanding the evolving landscape of education and its potential impact on students' learning experiences.

Write me a band 9 IELTS Writing task 2 for this question: In schools and universities, girls tend to choose arts, while boys like science. What are the reasons for this trend and do you think this tendency should be changed?

The preferences of girls leaning towards arts subjects while boys gravitate towards science disciplines in schools and universities have raised questions about the reasons behind this trend. Moreover, there is a debate regarding whether this tendency should be altered. In this essay, I will explore the underlying factors contributing to this phenomenon and provide my perspective on whether or not this trend should be changed.

Several factors can be attributed to the disparity in subject choices between girls and boys in educational institutions. Firstly, societal expectations and stereotypes play a significant role in shaping individuals' interests and career aspirations. From an early age, children are exposed to gender-based stereotypes that associate certain subjects with specific genders. Girls are often encouraged to pursue artistic endeavors, while boys are steered towards logical and scientific fields. This perpetuates the notion that arts are more suitable for girls and science for boys, resulting in a self-reinforcing cycle.

Additionally, differences in personal interests and aptitudes also contribute to this trend. Biological and neurological factors, although not definitive, can influence the way girls and boys approach and perceive certain subjects. For instance, studies have suggested that females tend to excel in verbal and creative domains, aligning with the arts, while males exhibit strengths in spatial and analytical abilities, aligning with scientific fields. These inherent differences, combined with societal expectations, can steer individuals towards certain subjects.

However, it is imperative to challenge and transcend these gender biases in subject choices. Rigid adherence to societal expectations limits individual freedom and prevents the full utilization of talents and potential. Encouraging girls to explore and pursue STEM (science, technology, engineering, and mathematics) subjects can lead to greater gender equality in traditionally male-dominated fields. Likewise, providing opportunities and support for boys interested in arts and humanities can foster diversity and promote well-rounded education.

To effect change, it is essential to address the root causes of this trend. Educational institutions should actively work toward debunking gender stereotypes through inclusive curriculum design, guidance counseling, and mentorship programs. By promoting gender-neutral subject choices and challenging societal expectations, educational institutions can create an environment that encourages students to pursue their passions and interests irrespective of gender.

In conclusion, the trend of girls leaning towards arts subjects and boys favoring science disciplines is influenced by societal expectations, stereotypes, and individual aptitudes. While inherent differences may contribute to this phenomenon, it is crucial to challenge and reshape these biases to create a more inclusive educational landscape. Encouraging girls to explore STEM subjects and providing support for boys interested in arts can lead to greater gender equality and diversified academic pursuits. By fostering an environment that encourages students to pursue their interests, regardless of traditional gender norms, education institutions can better prepare individuals for a more inclusive and progressive society.

Enhance my introduction: It is true that girls often lean towards art subjects, while boys often lean towards science subjects in schools and universities.There are several reasons behind this phenomenon, and I reckon that this trend should not be changed.

It is an undeniable truth that a significant trend exists in schools and universities, where girls tend to gravitate towards art subjects, while boys often lean towards science disciplines. The underlying reasons for this phenomenon are multifaceted, and from my perspective, it is my firm belief that this trend should not be altered. In the following essay, I will delve into the reasons contributing to this trend and provide a comprehensive analysis of why maintaining this inclination can be beneficial for individuals and society as a whole.
